In addition to overcoming the poor surgical bill in posterior side, we can avoid post-operative air leakage even in dense fissure.
Moreover, this technique is suitable for uniportal baths to perform smooth stapling because the angle of inserted stapler is limited.
After division of the first branch of the right pulmonary artery, the right upper branch was resurrected using section-guided stapling.
In this technique, the curved section was inserted between the right upper branch and upper lobe.
The section provided space to insert a stapler smoothly and protected the upper lobe from damage caused by misinsertion of the stapler.
In addition, the curved body of the suction provided space on the posterior side.
Therefore, the tip of the stapler was easily accessed on the monitor.
Finally, the right upper bronx was divided.
After division of the right upper bronx, the upper lobe vein was divided using the same approach.

Although suction-guided stapling is very useful for division of pulmonary vein or bronx, this technique should not be adapted for stapling the pulmonary artery because the wall of the pulmonary artery is fragile and can be ruptured by insertion of suction.
